# Private key negative corpus — should NOT trigger
-----BEGIN CERTIFICATE-----
-----BEGIN PUBLIC KEY-----
-----BEGIN RSA PUBLIC KEY-----
-----BEGIN CERTIFICATE REQUEST-----
-----BEGIN X509 CRL-----
-----BEGIN DH PARAMETERS-----
This is a PEM file header without content
BEGIN RSA PRIVATE KEY (no dashes)
- - - BEGIN RSA PRIVATE KEY - - - (wrong delimiter)
PRIVATE KEY without BEGIN header
Encoded data: MIIEpAIBAAKCAQEA0Z3VS5JJ (no PEM wrapper)
Public certificate: -----BEGIN CERTIFICATE-----\nMIIB...\n-----END CERTIFICATE-----
CSR file: -----BEGIN CERTIFICATE REQUEST-----\ndata\n-----END CERTIFICATE REQUEST-----
SSH public key: ssh-rsa AAAAB3NzaC1yc2E user@host
Public key: -----BEGIN PUBLIC KEY-----\nMIIBIjAN\n-----END PUBLIC KEY-----
RSA public: -----BEGIN RSA PUBLIC KEY-----\nMIIBCgKC\n-----END RSA PUBLIC KEY-----
DH params: -----BEGIN DH PARAMETERS-----\nMIGHAoGB\n-----END DH PARAMETERS-----
CRL: -----BEGIN X509 CRL-----\nMIIBpDCC\n-----END X509 CRL-----
Plain text private key reference (no PEM block)
Just the word BEGIN without the delimiter dashes
